verses.text: God shows Jeremiah two baskets of figs, one very good, the other rotten and inedible. A simple sign. God uses common sights to teach who truly stands with Him.
God shows Jeremiah two baskets of figs, one very good, the other rotten and inedible. A simple sign. God uses common sights to teach who truly stands with Him.
God shows Jeremiah two baskets of figs, one very good, the other rotten and inedible. A simple sign. God uses common sights to teach who truly stands with Him.

The good figs are the exiles in Babylon, whom God will own for good; 'I will give them an heart to know me,' and they shall return to Him. Affliction is their blessing. God turns captivity into the means of a new, knowing heart.

The good figs are the exiles in Babylon, whom God will own for good; 'I will give them an heart to know me,' and they shall return to Him. Affliction is their blessing. God turns captivity into the means of a new, knowing heart.

The good figs are the exiles in Babylon, whom God will own for good; 'I will give them an heart to know me,' and they shall return to Him. Affliction is their blessing. God turns captivity into the means of a new, knowing heart.
The bad figs are Zedekiah and those who stayed, whom God will give over to ruin and dispersion. Apparent safety was no blessing. Those left behind in their sin face the worse judgment.
